metadata.title: Local Debug 3-Flow Execution: Post-Training → Analysis → Biostatistics
metadata.priority: P0 — validates pipeline before GCP spend
metadata.monitoring: factorial-monitor skill for flow tracking + timing
metadata.estimated-time: 2-3 hours (DynUNet on local RTX 2070 Super for inference)

context.summary:
Run all 3 CPU-capable flows locally using DynUNet artifacts from dynunet_loss_variation_v2 experiment. This is the REAL test — not synthetic data, not mocks. Real checkpoints, real MiniVess volumes, real inference.
Purpose: Find and fix ALL emerging issues before spending $65+ on GCP.
Hardware: RTX 2070 Super (8 GB VRAM), 64 GB RAM, CUDA 12.6 Data: 70 MiniVess volumes locally at data/raw/minivess/ Checkpoints: 4 DynUNet runs × 7 checkpoints each (100 epochs)

verified-facts.fact: Checkpoint format: dict with model_state_dict key (44 tensors, DynUNet)
MiniVess data: 70 imagesTr + 70 labelsTr .nii.gz files available
_average_checkpoints uses model_state_dict key — MATCHES checkpoint format
SWA plugin (swa.py:65) uses state_dict key — MISMATCH, needs investigation
Tags backfilled: model_family, flow_name, fold_id, with_aux_calib, loss_function
Post-training smoke test passed: 4 runs created with correct tags

context.scope:
Flow 1: Post-Training — 2 losses × {none, swa} = 4 post-training variants Flow 2: Analysis — 4 variants evaluated on MiniVess validation (real inference!) Flow 3: Biostatistics — ANOVA on evaluation results
NOT in scope: DeepVess external test, zero-shot baselines, multi-fold, full 384 conditions. Those need 24 training conditions from GCP.

phase.name: Pre-Flight Verification
phase.description: Verify all prerequisites before running flows. Every check is a CLI command — no guessing, no assuming (CLAUDE.md Rule: query state programmatically).

task.title: Verify checkpoint loadability with correct key

tasks.task: Load ONE checkpoint, verify model_state_dict is loadable as DynUNet. Also check: does SWA plugin (swa.py) use state_dict or model_state_dict? If mismatch: fix SWA plugin OR _average_checkpoints to use consistent key. Time: record time to load one checkpoint.
Load ONE volume from data/raw/minivess/imagesTr/, verify shape and dtype. Time: record time to load one volume.
Check configs/splits/ for fold definitions. The evaluation needs train/val split assignments to know which volumes are validation.
Export all required env vars. Verify with echo.

task.title: Clean previous smoke test MLflow runs

tasks.task: Delete smoke_local_post_training experiment if it exists from the earlier test. Fresh start.

phase.timing: Record: checkpoint load time, volume load time

phase.name: Post-Training Flow (Layer B)
phase.description:
Run post-training on 2 DynUNet conditions (cbdice_cldice, dice_ce) with methods {none, swa}. Creates 4 MLflow runs.
This phase uses run_factorial_post_training() which we already smoke-tested. Now we verify the FULL flow including: - SWA checkpoint averaging with REAL weights (not synthetic) - MLflow run creation with all tags - Checkpoint file validity (loadable after averaging)

tasks.task: Load the SWA-averaged checkpoint. Verify: - Same number of parameters as original (44 tensors) - No NaN/Inf values in any tensor - File size is reasonable (~same as original)

phase.gate: 4 post-training runs with valid checkpoints and correct tags.
phase.timing: Record: total post-training time, per-method breakdown

phase.name: Analysis Flow (Layer C) — Real Inference
phase.description:
THIS IS THE CRITICAL PHASE. Runs actual DynUNet inference on MiniVess validation volumes using the RTX 2070 Super GPU.
The analysis flow must: 1. Discover the 4 post-training runs 2. Load DynUNet weights from checkpoints 3. Run sliding-window inference on validation volumes 4. Compute per-volume segmentation metrics 5. Log results to MLflow
Expected issues: - require_eval_metrics gate (pass False for these runs) - Data path configuration (Docker vs local paths) - Memory usage during inference - Inference speed (DynUNet on RTX 2070 Super ~few sec/volume)

tasks.task:
Call run_analysis_flow() pointing to smoke_local_post_training. Must pass require_eval_metrics=False since these are not "production-complete" runs with eval_fold2_dsc.
Build 2 ensemble strategies: per_loss_single_best, all_loss_all_best. Time: record total analysis time, per-volume inference time.
Query MLflow for eval/{fold}/vol/{vol_id}/{metric} metrics. Must have at least: dsc, cldice for each validation volume. Time: log metric count and logging time.
Check that ensemble averaging produces different predictions than individual models (logit-level averaging, not metric-level).
Fix issues as they emerge. Document each one in the report. Use failure triage protocol if multiple failures.

phase.gate: Analysis runs created with per-volume metrics. Ensembles evaluated.
phase.timing: Record: total analysis time, per-volume inference time, ensemble build time

phase.name: Biostatistics Flow (Layer D)
phase.description:
Run biostatistics on the analysis results. This exercises the NEWLY ADDED 6-factor pipeline code from today's session.
Must verify: - _compose_condition_key produces 6-part keys - _build_per_volume_data uses composite keys - task_compute_factorial_anova runs on real data - DuckDB populated with all columns

task.title: Run biostatistics flow on analysis results

tasks.task: Call run_biostatistics_flow() pointing to smoke_local_evaluation. Use BiostatisticsConfig from the smoke_local.yaml. Time: record total biostatistics time.

task.title: Verify DuckDB has all 6 factorial columns

tasks.task: Query DuckDB: SELECT model_family, loss_function, with_aux_calib, post_training_method, recalibration, ensemble_strategy FROM runs. All 6 columns must have non-null values.
Check that FactorialAnovaResult was produced. At minimum: p_values for loss_name factor (the only one varying with more than 1 level in this test).
Check PairwiseResult objects were produced for all condition pairs.

phase.gate: DuckDB has 6-factor data. ANOVA produced results. Pipeline end-to-end verified.
phase.timing: Record: total biostatistics time, ANOVA time, DuckDB build time

phase.name: Report + Fix Cycle
phase.description: Document all findings in the report. Fix any issues found. Run make test-staging to verify no regressions.

tasks.task: Write to docs/planning/run-debug-factorial-experiment-report-2nd-pass-local.md following the format of the 1st pass report. Include: timing, issues found, fixes applied, recommendations.
Verify all tests still pass after the local run.
Single commit with all fixes found during execution.

phase.gate: Report written. All tests pass. Ready for GCP.

execution-notes.note: Use /factorial-monitor for tracking flow execution status
Time every phase with Python time.perf_counter() or shell time command
MLflow should log fine-grained timing via mlflow.log_metric("phase_X_seconds", ...)
All flows run with MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1 (local, no Docker)
DynUNet inference on RTX 2070 Super: expect ~2-5 sec per 3D volume
Total data: ~23 validation volumes × 4 models = ~92 inference runs
